119624.explanation: Join in! Applies the reflection principle whereby the best qualified people occupy the right positions.

119625.explanation: Only for those subjects where YES or NO suffices. For all other situations, we engage in dialogue with residents. (kitchen table conversation)

119628.explanation: Yes, as long as it fits the surroundings.

119629.explanation: In addition to European, national, and provincial financial arrangements, the municipality mainly has a facilitating role, which also has financial value.

119632.explanation: A large asylum seekers' center does not fit in our municipality. For asylum seeker shelters, we aim for smaller locations.

119633.explanation: Where it fits on a small scale, we agree with the statement! In other cases, employers can look for a good and suitable alternative together with the municipality.

119637.explanation: A fireworks display, like carbide shooting, provides enjoyment and connection.

119641.explanation: Yes, as long as it fits within the zoning plan and the landscape.

119645.explanation: The schools already have their own resources for this.

119646.explanation: Yes, as a supplement to the efforts the association itself makes.

119649.explanation: Given the size of the municipality of Molenlanden and the need for enforcement, we need more community enforcement officers (boa’s).

119650.explanation: Given the size of the municipality of Molenlanden, this is not an effective measure.

119651.explanation: Yes, if there is a need.

119655.explanation: Yes, especially now that there is a tight housing market.

119657.explanation: Yes, we see this already happens frequently.

119658.explanation: We believe that everyone should contribute according to their ability to pay.

119668.explanation: Only if it demonstrably stimulates employment and takes into account the surroundings, sustainability, and livability, do we see possibilities.

119669.explanation: Yes, the implementation of this lies with the entrepreneur.

119674.explanation: Yes, we do this for low-income earners via the MeeDoen fund and that must continue.

119677.explanation: Yes, every resident has the right to a decent home.

119678.explanation: Yes, those are important meeting places for residents.

119681.explanation: Yes, by banning truck traffic in the villages. But we cannot decide this for the N-roads within the municipality, as those belong to the province.

119682.explanation: Yes, small-scale shelter fitting the villages.

119685.explanation: Yes, that contributes to climate adaptation.

119624.explanation: It's about the quality of the aldermen. That is more important than an equal distribution.

119625.explanation: The SGP is a supporter of representative democracy in which elected representatives on behalf of the people control the executive board.

119628.explanation: We support initiatives on the farmyard, but they must comply with regulations. We do not want to always allow them just like that.

119629.explanation: It is important that initiatives come from society. As a municipality, we must support them.

119632.explanation: We prefer small-scale shelter that fits the villages rather than a large-scale asylum seekers' center in one of the villages.

119633.explanation: This must not come at the expense of the already tight housing market.

119637.explanation: We facilitate initiatives that come from society, but as a government, we do not actively take the initiative.

119641.explanation: The SGP supports good policy to redevelop vacant agricultural buildings. This could include building locations near villages.

119645.explanation: There are already various arrangements that make this possible.

119646.explanation: It's important that sports clubs manage their finances well, just like any other association.

119649.explanation: We will continue to work towards more neighborhood officers and community enforcement officers with extended authority. We also want at least one youth boa for our municipality.

119650.explanation: In unsafe places, this is a good measure to apply.

119651.explanation: It is important to provide a place where people can go if home is no longer safe.

119655.explanation: In some places in the municipality, this is possible. We consider the possibilities per location.

119657.explanation: Regarding benefits, it seems valuable to challenge people to contribute to society. The SGP wants to explore the possibilities for this.

119658.explanation: When taxes cause debts, we support debt forgiveness. In other situations, we do not.

119661.explanation: The SGP advocates a parking policy that allows residents to charge their electric cars at home, enabling them to use their own solar panels.

119662.explanation: It is important to carefully consider this in the design of our centers and to provide customized solutions where possible.

119663.explanation: The SGP is a strong supporter of freedom of choice, so that a resident can choose the healthcare provider that suits him or her.

119668.explanation: Where possible, we will certainly support that. Think, for example, of vacant agricultural buildings.

119669.explanation: Sunday is a special day for the SGP: the first day of the week, when we come together around His Word. Therefore, we remain committed to preserving Sunday rest, do not support the expansion of the number of shopping Sundays, and urge reduction where possible.

119674.explanation: This is the responsibility of parents. For parents who cannot afford it, funds are available through various foundations to ensure these children can still receive swimming lessons.

119677.explanation: On the current tight housing market, all housing units are welcome!

119678.explanation: This is not a goal in itself. However, we do strive to maintain existing facilities.

119681.explanation: We commit ourselves to keeping disruptive freight traffic that has no business in the centers out.

119682.explanation: We host refugees on a small scale where possible. It is important that this process is carried out in consultation with the community.

119685.explanation: New neighborhoods must have sufficient green space. It is important to look at what is possible per location.

119624.explanation: An equal number of men and women is not a goal in itself. For the dynamics within a municipal executive, it is good to have a certain degree of diversity.

119625.explanation: A referendum almost always simplifies the choice at hand. In very exceptional cases, a referendum may be suitable. We do want strong community involvement in the decision-making on important issues.

119628.explanation: Ancillary activities in the countryside serve multiple purposes: a future-proof agricultural sector and strengthening recreation and tourism with the experience of peace, space, and nature. There are conditions, but we approach initiatives that strengthen the countryside with a positive attitude.

119629.explanation: Financial support does not seem the right form of help in this case. However, we encourage these kinds of initiatives, offer other forms of assistance, and think along. We are willing to incur the costs involved. In addition, national subsidies are available.

119632.explanation: We do not want a large asylum seekers' center. We prefer several small-scale locations with about 20 asylum seekers each. This prevents disturbing the balance in a village. We do not reject hosting. The Distribution Act also obliges us to. But we must do so wisely and carefully.

119633.explanation: We do not want our scarce homes to go to labor migrants. That is why we have a policy that labor migrants can be housed on industrial estates.

119637.explanation: The CDA parliamentary group in the House of Representatives has ensured, among other things, that neighborhood associations may organize fireworks shows. We gladly encourage this! A fireworks show fosters connection. Let us empower communities with this; it may help prevent possible escalations.

119641.explanation: We do not want to turn farmers into project developers. Agricultural land can sometimes be used for housing, but that is not a matter for general policy. The municipality must actively make strategic choices.

119645.explanation: We encourage music education in schools. We do this in cooperation with local music associations. Music lessons must be accessible to every child. Music is good for personal development and is a unifying force in society.

119646.explanation: We primarily ensure this is not necessary. We do not simply abandon sports clubs either. They are too important for that. Together we seek a solution. The municipality already provides a lot of funding to sports clubs through the accommodation policy.

119649.explanation: Neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers are insufficiently visible in the villages. Soon we will have a second enforcement officer in the municipality. That is still too few. As far as we are concerned, the staff formation should be increased.

119650.explanation: We do not want to become a surveillance state. We should mainly invest in neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers. This way you get human supervision. They are points of contact for various issues and provide assistance.

119651.explanation: Domestic violence unfortunately occurs in Molenlanden as well. Our sparsely populated area is not a logical location for a women's shelter. Residents must know how and where to find this help. This must also be low-threshold and clearly visible on our municipal page!

119655.explanation: This is just lip service. Enough homes must be built. At locations with soft soil, however, we can consider park-like residential neighborhoods with light building materials and few paved roads. Think of Nieuw-Lekkerland East.

119657.explanation: It is wonderful if people do socially meaningful work, but we do not want to make this a condition. That would lead to extra control and supervision. We have had enough of that.

119658.explanation: The municipality sometimes waives waste disposal and sewer charges for people with income below social assistance levels. Out of solidarity, we continue this until a better support plan is in place. We strive for one clear, transparent scheme.

119661.explanation: Of course, but only upon request. But without charging points, there are no electric cars.

119662.explanation: The speed limit is already 30 km/h in almost all places. We want to keep rules clear and simple.

119663.explanation: Care is about people and must be appropriate, including being in line with someone's convictions. This is especially important for long-term care. Think, for example, of choosing a Christian nursing home!

119668.explanation: There is significant demand from local entrepreneurs for space in business parks close to the centers. New homes can be built in the vacant spots in the centers.

119669.explanation: It is important to have a shared weekly rhythm to some extent. Sunday is historically and culturally the most logical day to choose for rest, reflection, and relationships. The world is already hectic enough.

119674.explanation: Every child must learn to swim. In some villages, school swimming in cooperation with the pool is obvious. This also supports the pools. Elsewhere, school swimming is not obvious. Then we seek customized solutions so that the approximately 5% of children without a diploma in our municipality learn to swim.

119677.explanation: There is considerable demand for small living spaces for single starters. If renting studios can meet this need, we welcome it.

119678.explanation: Nothing is mandatory, but the wish of a village to have a village center is understandable and the municipality would do well to cooperate. It benefits the village community and social encounters there.

119681.explanation: Banning is harsh, but regulating certainly. Our ribbon developments and centers are not designed for heavy (freight) traffic. These are meant for the highways and provincial roads.

119682.explanation: Absolutely agree! Locations with about 100 refugees strain the capacity of a single (village) center. That is why we want locations of about 20 refugees spread across the municipality. This keeps the (village) center balanced and the hosting bearable. It empowers the community.

119685.explanation: We are in favor of a lot of green in neighborhoods, but these kinds of generic requirements are unwise. 20% could be a guideline from which motivated deviations may be made.

119624.explanation: The Christian Union wants a municipal executive with competent, honest aldermen who form a strong administration together.

119625.explanation: The Christian Union does not support referendums. The elected representatives (municipal council) are responsible for decision-making. However, we do support the use of TIP Molenlanden. Additionally, in Molenlanden it is possible to start a citizen initiative.

119628.explanation: We want to give farmers the space to develop side activities. To supplement their income, but also to shorten the food chain and strengthen tourism.

119629.explanation: We want the municipality to think along and assist with knowledge and expertise.

119632.explanation: We want to offer a safe place to people fleeing war, violence, or persecution. We support small-scale shelters that fit the capacity of the respective community.

119633.explanation: Nice if possible, but we also want the municipality to look for one or more central locations where labor migrants can live. We stand for good housing and at the same time as little pressure as possible on the local housing stock.

119637.explanation: We want it to be possible for communities/associations to take this up themselves through (flexible) permits. We see a facilitating role for the municipality.

119641.explanation: A temporary home on the property is allowed for a son or daughter who wants to take over the business in the future. Building extra homes on the property not just like that, because it could also endanger the permits of nearby farms.

119645.explanation: Currently, all primary school children receive music lessons at school. Part of the costs are borne by the municipality. We want to continue with this.

119646.explanation: Sports clubs are very important for the vitality and livability of our communities. Clubs already receive support. We want to continue this. If sports clubs face financial difficulties, the municipality will work with the club to find a solution.

119649.explanation: Molenlanden is a safe municipality. We want to maintain that. An additional enforcement officer is desirable, especially for traffic safety.

119650.explanation: Cameras are installed in some (unsafe) places in Molenlanden. That is acceptable, but not at more locations arbitrarily. Moreover, it is difficult with such a large municipality. Camera surveillance is very expensive and experience shows it offers little actual safety.

119651.explanation: We strongly focus on protecting the vulnerable, including victims of domestic violence. Everyone has the right to a safe environment. Molenlanden victims can currently go to surrounding municipalities. If there is insufficient capacity, Molenlanden itself must provide shelter.

119655.explanation: To combat the housing shortage, all options must be used. It is important that the homes are of good quality. We do not want people to live in deplorable conditions.

119657.explanation: We find it important that people on social assistance participate in society where possible. Mandatory duties are not a goal in themselves. It is about offering opportunities and support so everyone can participate to the best of their ability.

119658.explanation: It must remain possible to waive municipal taxes if people are in financial difficulty.

119661.explanation: Before the municipality designates parking spaces, there must be sufficient need. If that need exists, we want to be generous in designating new parking spaces. We do take the current parking pressure in the neighborhood into account.

119662.explanation: A nice goal, but (not yet) possible everywhere. Experience shows that the layout must be suitable to actually drive 30 km/h. Otherwise, it is not useful and enforcement proves difficult.

119663.explanation: That space must exist and remain. A condition is that the healthcare provider at least meets minimum quality requirements.

119668.explanation: Local entrepreneurs are of great social importance. They get space to grow in existing business parks. Expansion must not come at the expense of the livability of the nearby community.

119669.explanation: We find it important that there is one day per week that is not focused on work and consumption. Sunday should be a day for rest, encounter, and reflection.

119674.explanation: School swimming is difficult to organize in Molenlanden because most communities do not have a pool nearby. We do want to offer parents financial support because we think it is important that children obtain a swimming diploma. Finances should not be a reason to miss out on a diploma.

119677.explanation: To combat the housing shortage, it must also be possible to rent out part of a home. We also support splitting (large) homes into smaller homes. At the same time, we must guard against overcrowded buildings (e.g., with labor migrants).

119678.explanation: It is important that every community in Molenlanden has a village house function. That can be a school, MFA (multi-functional accommodation), healthcare facility, etc.

119681.explanation: We support stimulating other (detour) routes, especially for heavy traffic. Local entrepreneurs must still be able to reach their businesses.

119682.explanation: We prefer an equal distribution over the communities. At the same time, that is not realistic when looking at national regulations and financing.

119685.explanation: Green spaces and trees are important. A green environment is fortunately present in many places in Molenlanden. At least 20% is a nice goal, but as an obligation, it is too strict and possibly not achievable if we want to build affordably. We do ask for attention to climate adaptation in every project.

119624.explanation: It is good if men and women form a college, but this does not have to be in a specific ratio.

119625.explanation: Residents have many opportunities to express their opinion to the council, including initiative proposals, citizen surveys, and open podiums.

119629.explanation: If residents want to generate green energy together, the municipality can cooperate as a partner, but not solely as a financier.

119632.explanation: The Distribution Act also gives Molenlanden the task to open a reception center for asylum seekers (AZC), but sufficient funding must come from the national government.

119633.explanation: Seasonal workers have the right to fully adequate housing. Molenlanden should not be saddled with extra supervision tasks to prevent abuses.

119637.explanation: If residents themselves can take an initiative via their village council, the municipality can grant permission.

119641.explanation: This is a discussion between the municipality and the province. The province should give Molenlanden space for this.

119645.explanation: Cultural education consists of more than music. No subsidies are given for dance, painting, etc. We do not see this as a municipal task.

119646.explanation: Sports clubs must be able to financially support themselves.

119649.explanation: More police means more safety. Enforcement officers (boa's) do not have sufficient mandate to increase the feeling of safety.

119650.explanation: If the police advise placing cameras with a purpose, this must indeed be done.

119651.explanation: Femicide must receive attention. Connecting to support for victims must be well arranged.

119655.explanation: According to us, this does not apply in our municipality.

119657.explanation: In our municipality, the Participation Act is implemented by Avres. They ensure that everyone is accounted for. People should be seen as human beings.

119658.explanation: This regulation serves a purpose: to help the vulnerable in society.

119661.explanation: We believe that this should be requested by the residents themselves.

119662.explanation: Not applicable. The entire built-up area already has a 30 km/h speed limit.

119663.explanation: Respecting personal choice is important, but ensuring that care is available is most important.

119668.explanation: An expansion must be based on a sound business model.

119669.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneur whether they make use of the shopping Sunday.

119674.explanation: Swimming lessons, like music lessons, are the parents' own responsibility. Financial support can be provided if needed through learning allowance.

119677.explanation: This must be assessed on a case-by-case basis.

119678.explanation: Each village must be looked at to determine what is needed.

119681.explanation: Banning freight traffic may be necessary in some centers. With two provincial roads, this is impossible.

119682.explanation: Refugee sheltering must be concentrated in a few locations to ensure cost-efficiency for the municipality.

119685.explanation: The requirements to obtain an environmental permit already include enough rules regarding greenery. A percentage is too general.

119624.explanation: Society consists of men and women. The executive board should reflect society as much as possible.

119625.explanation: There are issues that will only arise during a council term about which voters could not have judged. There must be an additional decision point.

119628.explanation: This must be assessed per situation and location.

119629.explanation: We consider the generation of sustainable energy important. The municipality must facilitate local energy cooperatives. Residents should benefit (financially) from this. However, structural subsidies should not be at the expense of society.

119632.explanation: We are open to the establishment of asylum seekers' centers, provided they are appropriate for the scale of our villages.

119633.explanation: Employers must provide appropriate housing. It must be socially and morally responsible. Not every location is suitable for this. Too much residential dependence on an employer often leads to problems and is undesirable.

119637.explanation: We support a fireworks ban for private individuals. In our view, centralized fireworks shows are a good alternative.

119641.explanation: The normal permit/zoning plan procedures must be followed for this. We want to prevent uncontrolled development.

119645.explanation: This is a skill everyone must learn. It should be a standard part of the curriculum.

119646.explanation: The association must meet a need. The municipality has a reviewing role.

119649.explanation: Crime is also increasing in our villages. And we must combat that.

119650.explanation: Depending on the situation, camera surveillance may or may not be applied.

119651.explanation: Everyone must be able to feel safe. Much violence against women still takes place at home.

119655.explanation: The design and infrastructure of such homes in a park are not intended for permanent residence. On the other hand, the housing need is urgent. To be reviewed from park to park.

119657.explanation: We believe this must be tailored individually.

119658.explanation: Residents who truly cannot afford it must be able to have their taxes waived.

119661.explanation: The trend is electric driving and this must be facilitated.

119662.explanation: It is proven that there are fewer accidents at 30 km/h. Roads must then be designed accordingly.

119663.explanation: The primary focus is delivering care. Only when more options exist can there be customization.

119668.explanation: Existing companies with many traffic movements are encouraged to relocate to business parks along main roads. Expansion of existing industrial areas must be well integrated into the landscape.

119669.explanation: Currently, there are no shopping Sundays in the Molenlanden municipality. We are in favor of introducing them.

119674.explanation: Every child in this water-rich municipality should be able to swim at an adequate level.

119677.explanation: We do not want an unlimited number of people with different backgrounds living in homes.

119678.explanation: We want every village to have its own meeting place.

119681.explanation: The safety of residents is a priority, but damage to homes must also be prevented.

119682.explanation: Preferably, we would like this arranged per village. Considering efficiency, this is not feasible.

119685.explanation: Greenery has a positive effect on the living environment. Furthermore, it also helps reduce heat stress.

statements.title: Molenlanden should actively ban through truck traffic within the municipal boundaries.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety should be deployed in Molenlanden.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Residents of Molenlanden should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Molenlanden moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
There should be more camera surveillance in Molenlanden.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: Molenlanden should distribute refugee accommodation across all villages in the municipality.
Molenlanden should establish a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Industrial estates in Molenlanden may be allowed to expand.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should require that in all new construction projects at least 20% of the land be used for trees and plants.
